name: Ek Chuah
description: Ek Chuah, also known as Ek Chuaj, is a prominent deity in Mayan mythology, primarily associated with merchants, cacao, and warfare. He is considered a powerful patron of merchants and travelers, ensuring their safety and success in their ventures. Ek Chuah is often depicted with a black face, a bundle on his back representing the goods he protects, and sometimes a scorpion's tail. His role extends beyond commerce, as he is also linked to cacao, a valuable commodity in Mayan society used in rituals and as currency. This association highlights the deity's importance in the economic and social fabric of the Mayan civilization. While primarily a benevolent figure for traders, Ek Chuah also possesses a warrior aspect, protecting his followers from harm and ensuring victory in battles. Some accounts suggest he is also associated with the underworld, further solidifying his complex and multifaceted nature. His worship involved rituals and offerings, particularly by merchants seeking his blessings for prosperous trade routes and successful transactions. Ek Chuah's influence permeated various aspects of Mayan life, reflecting the significance of trade, agriculture, and warfare in their society. He embodies the duality of prosperity and protection, making him a vital deity in the Mayan pantheon.
subtitle: Merchant God of Cacao and Warfare

keyMyths.title: Ek Chuah's Protection of Merchants
keyMyths.description: Ek Chuah is said to guide merchants through treacherous paths, protecting them from bandits and ensuring profitable trades. Merchants often offered him cacao and incense before embarking on long journeys.
keyMyths.source: Various Mayan codices and historical accounts

keyMyths.title: The Gift of Cacao
keyMyths.description: Legend tells of Ek Chuah bestowing the gift of cacao upon the Mayan people, recognizing its economic and ritualistic value. This act solidified his importance within the culture.
keyMyths.source: Popol Vuh interpretations and archaeological findings

keyMyths.title: Ek Chuah's Warring Aspect
keyMyths.description: In times of war, Ek Chuah was invoked to grant warriors strength and victory. He was seen as a protector, ensuring the safety of his followers in battle.
keyMyths.source: Depictions in Mayan art and historical records

sources.author: Michael D. Coe
sources.description: Overview of Mayan civilization, including religious beliefs.
sources.text: Overview of Mayan civilization, including religious beliefs.
